data.title: Lagarde says ‘impossible’ for the ECB to always meet the 2% inflation target
data.description:
Inflation fears have surpassed US borders. Christine Lagarde, president of the European Central Bank (ECB), warned that abrupt changes in global trade and the defense system of the region will make it more difficult to maintain inflation stability. Christine Lagarde has said that the changes are causing big shocks to the eurozone economy. These shocks […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/lagarde-hard-ecb-to-meet-2-inflation-target/

data.title: Cardano has lost $15B since Trump reneged on Strategic Reserve promise
data.description:
On March 2, Charles Hoskinson and his Cardano community believed Donald Trump would follow through on his presidential promise. He didn't.
The post Cardano has lost $15B since Trump reneged on Strategic Reserve promise appeared first on Protos.
source: https://protos.com/cardano-has-lost-15b-since-trump-reneged-on-strategic-reserve-promise/

data.description:

data.title: Hearing of US House Finance Committee examines stablecoin bills, CBDC opposition
data.description:
Crypto assets were under scrutiny in the U.S. Congress on March 11. The House of Representatives Financial Services Committee debated the merits of stablecoins and a central bank digital currency (CBDC) in a hearing titled “Navigating the Digital Payments Ecosystem: Examining a Federal Framework for Payment Stablecoins and Consequences of a U.S. Central Bank Digital […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/us-house-finance-committee-stablecoin-bills/

data.title: Inflation Data Unlikely To Prompt Fed Rate Cut, Economist Says
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, TS Lombard's Chief U.S. Economist Steven Blitz stated on March 13 that recent inflation data does not signal a need for the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates. Although the Consumer Price Index (CPI) decreased from 3% to 2.8% in February, Blitz noted that anomalies within the data cast doubt on any attempts to interpret this as a trend.

Blitz highlighted that the annual growth rate of goods prices, excluding food and energy, adjusted for seasonality, was 2.7% in February, an improvement from January's 3.5%, yet still unstable. He remarked that this category is where the impact of tariffs is most evident in the first round. Ultimately, as employment rates continue to rise, inflation is expected to follow suit.
